vec_UnalignedPointer Struct Reference
[Unaligned Data Access]

An object corresponding to an unaligned pointer. More...

#include <vec_ops.h>


Data Fields

const uint64_t * ptr
const void * align

Detailed Description

An object corresponding to an unaligned pointer.

Because it contains both an aligned pointer and the original alignment information, it can be dereferenced more efficiently.

The 'ptr' field of this object is always an aligned pointer, equal to the truncated version of the possibly unaligned pointer passed to vec_init_unaligned_pointer. It can be modified as long as the new value is also equal to zero mod 8. For example, suppose you initialized this object with the unaligned pointer X and wanted to dereference the unaligned pointer X + 16. Since X + 16 has the same alignment as X, you can reuse this alignment object simply by:

my_struct.ptr += 2; // add 2 uint64_t == 16 bytes

The alignment information remains valid.

Field Documentation

const uint64_t* vec_UnalignedPointer::ptr

Aligned pointer to memory.

const void* vec_UnalignedPointer::align

Initial pointer, used for dblalign.

The documentation for this struct was generated from the following file:
